issue.title: Spec: Glyph-Level Side-Channel Annotations on `Word`

issue.body:
## Backfill

Retroactive feature-request issue for spec #29 (glyph-level annotations).
Filed as part of R2 Phase B to close the chain-backfill gap workspace-rc
left open.

## Feature

Add a parallel annotation structure to `Word` that records glyph-level
facts about the printed page (ligatures, long-s substitutions, swash forms,
etc.) **without** mutating the canonical / semantic ground-truth string.

This is the **foundation-library half** of a workspace-wide feature needed
by `pd-ocr-synth`, `pd-ocr-trainer`, `pd-ocr-labeler`, and
`pd-ocr-labeler-spa`.

## Spec

See #29 / `pd-book-tools/docs/specs/05-glyph-annotations.md`.

## Triage status

Pre-approved by virtue of having a written spec. Skipping /triage; the spec
issue (#29) is the canonical child.

Spec: docs/specs/05-glyph-annotations.md
issue.title: Glyph-Level Side-Channel Annotations on Word

issue.body:
Filed from the pd-book-tools deep code/security review.

Report: `docs/research/2026-05-22-deep-code-security-review.md`
Finding: #29

Moved from mistakenly filed meta issue: ConcaveTrillion/ocr-container-meta#322

Evidence:

- `pd_book_tools/ocr/word.py:131`
- `pd_book_tools/ocr/block.py:227`

`Word.__init__` stores `ground_truth_match_keys` by reference, and `Block.__init__` stores `additional_block_attributes` by reference.

Impact:

- Caller mutation after construction changes model state unexpectedly.

Remediation:

- Copy or deep-copy incoming metadata dicts.
- Add aliasing tests.

---
This issue was filed from a read-only review. Confirm behavior with a focused failing test before implementation where practical.
issue.title: pd-book-tools: constructors retain caller-owned mutable dicts
